messages.content: ugh my back is killing me, turning 41 next month and my body is letting me know lol

Ha, the body does have a way of sending those little birthday reminders! 😄

What's going on with your back - is it something specific like sitting too long, or just general achiness? Evening time can be rough if you've been on your feet or at a desk all day.
spent the whole weekend assembling furniture for my daughter's dorm, she starts college in the fall

Oh wow, that's a big milestone - both the furniture assembly marathon AND your daughter heading off to college! No wonder your back is protesting, those flat-pack furniture pieces are basically a full workout. All that bending, lifting, and contorting into weird positions... 😅

How are you feeling about her heading off to college? That's a pretty huge transition for the whole family.
my wife keeps telling me to stretch first and i keep not listening, classic

Ha, classic indeed! There's something very human about knowing exactly what we should do and just... not doing it 😄 Your wife is going to have a very satisfying "I told you so" moment when you finally start stretching regularly.

But honestly, it might be worth actually listening to her on this one - especially going into your 40s, the recovery time after stuff like that just gets longer. A few minutes of stretching beforehand would have been way less painful than a whole evening of a sore back!

How's your daughter feeling about starting college - excited, nervous, mix of both?
anyway we're doing a little anniversary dinner friday, 15 years. trying to pick somewhere decent downtown

15 years, congrats! And a nice dinner after a weekend of furniture assembly teamwork - you two earned it 😄

What kind of food are you usually into? And are you thinking more cozy and intimate, or a livelier spot with a good atmosphere? Also do you have a budget range in mind or going all out for the occasion?

I don't know your city so I can't make specific recommendations, but I can help you think through what to look for or what questions to ask!
